caption: Attestation
name: attestation
description: A cryptographic attestation that provides integrity, authenticity, and non-repudiation for an event record. Each signature is computed over a canonical serialization of the event record, thereby binding all included fields to a verifiable identity. A single attestation may carry more than one signature (e.g., a co-signature, notary, or witness over the same record). Optionally, the attestation may also support tamper-evident sequencing.

signatures.description: One or more digital signatures over the canonical serialization of the event record, each bound to a verifiable identity. The first is typically the producer; additional entries carry co-signatures such as a notary or witness over the same record. The signing algorithm, certificate or public-key material, and signing time are carried within each digital_signature object.

entry_hash.caption: Entry Hash
entry_hash.description: Fingerprint of this record's canonical serialization. Linked to by the next entry's prev_entry_hash to form a tamper-evident chain.

prev_entry_hash.caption: Previous Entry Hash
prev_entry_hash.description: Fingerprint of the prior record in the chain, providing tamper-evident ordering. Absent on the genesis entry of a chain.

chain_uid.caption: Chain ID
chain_uid.description: Identifier of the forensic chain or append-only log this record belongs to. Stable for the lifetime of the chain.
